title: Jagrantia monstrum
taxonomy: Jagrantia monstrum is a genus of extinct plants from the Paleozoic Era. The classification of Jagrantia monstrum places it within the group of vascular plants, specifically as a member of the clade Rhyniophyta. This group is further divided into the subdivision Zosterophyllidae. Jagrantia monstrum is one of the earliest known plants to exhibit vascular tissue, a characteristic that allowed for more complex growth and development. Its classification as a member of the Rhyniophyta highlights its position as a key transitional organism in the evolution of land plants.
